steps.title: Set up the two cases from the absolute value
steps.content:
For $2|3x - 1| = 4x + 9$, the absolute value creates two scenarios based on the sign of $(3x - 1)$:
  • Case 1: If $3x - 1 \geq 0$, then $2(3x - 1) = 4x + 9$
  • Case 2: If $3x - 1 < 0$, then $2(-(3x - 1)) = 4x + 9$

steps.title: Find the sum of both solutions
steps.content:
Add $\frac{11}{2}$ and $-\frac{7}{10}$ using a common denominator of 10:
$$\frac{11}{2} + \left(-\frac{7}{10}\right) = \frac{55}{10} - \frac{7}{10} = \frac{48}{10} = \frac{24}{5}$$
The sum of the solutions is $\frac{24}{5}$ or $4.8$.
The answer is 4.8,24/5,48/10.
